clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Evaluate stress reduction techniques (transcendental meditation, breathing control, yoga) as adjunct interventions for BP management
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with or without hypertension seeking non-pharmacological adjuncts to lifestyle or medication interventions for elevated BP or hypertension
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend stress reduction as adjunct therapy with weak recommendation (COR 2b) based on moderate-quality RCT evidence (LOE B-R)

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for stress reduction assessment
Assess current stress levels and BP status
Select technique: transcendental meditation, breathing control, or yoga
Screen for any contraindications to stress reduction techniques
Show COR 2b, LOE B-R recommendation with phrasing
Provide guidance on technique frequency and duration
Schedule BP re-assessment after stress reduction implementation

validationRules: BP values if provided must be within valid range
Patient must be adult (implied by guideline population)

rules: Assign LOE B-R (Moderate-quality evidence from 1 or more RCTs or meta-analyses of moderate-quality RCTs)
Apply to both hypertensive and normotensive adults
Recommend alongside lifestyle or medication interventions, not as replacement
Transcendental meditation, breathing control, and yoga are equally classified under same COR/LOE

categories.name: Stress Reduction - Transcendental Meditation
categories.role: adjunct to lifestyle or medication interventions

categories.name: Stress Reduction - Breathing Control/Yoga
categories.role: adjunct to lifestyle or medication interventions

role.value: adjunct to lifestyle or medication interventions

thresholds.sbp: N/A - stress reduction is not for acute crisis

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: N/A - stress reduction is adjunctive, not emergency intervention

appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing - position as adjunct only

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Consider stress reduction as adjunct to lifestyle or medication interventions; not a replacement for established treatments
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Re-assess BP after 8-12 weeks of consistent stress reduction practice
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP periodically to evaluate adjunct benefit
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag if patient attempts to use stress reduction as monotherapy for hypertension without lifestyle/medication interventions
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log stress reduction technique recommended, COR 2b LOE B-R, adjunct status
